# services.dovecot2

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.dovecot2`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.dovecot2.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Config file used for the whole dovecot configuration. |
| `services.dovecot2.createMailUser` | `boolean` | Whether to enable automatically creating the user given in {option}`services.dovecot2.settings.mail_uid` and the group given in {option}`services.dovecot2.settings.mail_gid`. |
| `services.dovecot2.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the dovecot 2.x POP3/IMAP server. |
| `services.dovecot2.enablePAM` | `boolean` | Whether to enable creating a own Dovecot PAM service and configure PAM user logins. |
| `services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox` | `list of (submodule)` | Configure Sieve filtering rules on IMAP actions ::: {.note} This option is no longer used starting from Dovecot version 2.4. ::: |
| `services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.*.after` | `null or absolute path` | When an IMAP event of interest occurs, this sieve script is executed after any user script respectively. This setting each specify the location of a single sieve script. The semantics of this setting is similar to sieve_after: the specified scripts form a sequence together with the user script in which the next script is only executed when an (implicit) keep action is executed. |
| `services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.*.before` | `null or absolute path` | When an IMAP event of interest occurs, this sieve script is executed before any user script respectively. This setting each specify the location of a single sieve script. The semantics of this setting is similar to sieve_before: the specified scripts form a sequence together with the user script in which the next script is only executed when an (implicit) keep action is executed. |
| `services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.*.causes` | `list of (one of "APPEND", "COPY", "FLAG")` | Only execute the administrator Sieve scripts for the mailbox configured with services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.<name>.name when one of the listed IMAPSIEVE causes apply. This has no effect on the user script, which is always executed no matter the cause. |
| `services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.*.from` | `null or string` | Only execute the administrator Sieve scripts for the mailbox configured with services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.<name>.name when the message originates from the indicated mailbox. This setting supports wildcards with a syntax compatible with the IMAP LIST command, meaning that this setting can apply to multiple or even all ("*") mailboxes. |
| `services.dovecot2.imapsieve.mailbox.*.name` | `string` | This setting configures the name of a mailbox for which administrator scripts are configured. The settings defined hereafter with matching sequence numbers apply to the mailbox named by this setting. This setting supports wildcards with a syntax compatible with the IMAP LIST command, meaning that this setting can apply to multiple or even all ("*") mailboxes. |
| `services.dovecot2.includeFiles` | `list of absolute path` | Files to include in the Dovecot config file using !include directives. |
| `services.dovecot2.mailPlugins` | `submodule` | Additional entries to add to the mail_plugins variable, globally and per protocol |
| `services.dovecot2.mailPlugins.globally` | `submodule` | Additional entries to add to the mail_plugins variable for all protocols |
| `services.dovecot2.mailPlugins.globally.enable` | `list of string` | mail plugins to enable as a list of strings to append to the top-level `$mail_plugins` configuration variable |
| `services.dovecot2.mailPlugins.perProtocol` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Additional entries to add to the mail_plugins variable, per protocol |
| `services.dovecot2.mailPlugins.perProtocol.<name>.enable` | `list of string` | mail plugins to enable as a list of strings to append to the corresponding per-protocol `$mail_plugins` configuration variable |
| `services.dovecot2.package` | `package` | The dovecot package to use. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (Dovecot config value)` | Dovecot configuration, see <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#all-dovecot-settings> for all available options. For information on the configuration structure, see <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/settings/syntax.html>. ::: {.warning} Explicit settings in [{option}`services.dovecot2.settings`](#opt-services.dovecot2.settings) can silently override values set by other `services.dovecot2.*` options. ::: |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."passdb pam"` | `null or (open submodule of attribute set of (Dovecot config value))` | Configuration for the PAM password database. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/config/auth/databases/pam.html>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."passdb pam".args` | `null or (list of string)` | Arguments for the passdb backend. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.3. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/2.3/configuration_manual/authentication/password_databases_passdb/#passdb-setting>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."passdb pam".driver` | `null or string` | The driver used for this password database. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#passdb_driver>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."passdb pam".failure_show_msg` | `null or boolean` | Replace the default "Authentication failed" reply with PAM's failure. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.4. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#passdb_pam_failure_show_msg>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."passdb pam".service_name` | `null or string` | The PAM service name to be used with the pam passdb. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.4. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#passdb_pam_service_name>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."userdb passwd"` | `null or (open submodule of attribute set of (Dovecot config value))` | Configuration for the Passwd user database. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/config/auth/databases/passwd.html>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings."userdb passwd".driver` | `null or string` | The driver used for this user database. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#userdb_driver>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.auth_mechanisms` | `(attribute set of boolean) or list of string` | Here you can supply a space-separated list of the authentication mechanisms you wish to use. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#auth_mechanisms>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.base_dir` | `absolute path` | The base directory in which Dovecot should store runtime data. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#base_dir>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.default_internal_group` | `string` | Define the default internal group. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#default_internal_group>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.default_internal_user` | `string` | Define the default internal user. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#default_internal_user>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.dovecot_config_version` | `null or string` | Dovecot configuration version. It uses the same versioning as Dovecot in general, e.g. 3.0.5. It specifies the configuration syntax, the used setting names and the expected default values. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#dovecot_config_version>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.dovecot_storage_version` | `null or string` | Dovecot storage file format version. It uses the same versioning as Dovecot in general, e.g. 3.0.5. It specifies the oldest Dovecot version that must be able to read files written by this Dovecot instance. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#dovecot_storage_version>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.mail_plugin_dir` | `absolute path` | The directory in which to search for Dovecot mail plugins. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#mail_plugin_dir>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.maildir_copy_with_hardlinks` | `boolean` | If enabled, copying of a message is done with hard links whenever possible. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#maildir_copy_with_hardlinks>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.plugin` | `null or (open submodule of attribute set of (Dovecot config value))` | Plugin settings. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.3. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.plugin.sieve_extensions` | `null or (list of string)` | The Sieve language extensions available to users. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.3. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/2.3/settings/pigeonhole/#pigeonhole_setting-sieve_extensions>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.plugin.sieve_global_extensions` | `null or (list of string)` | Which Sieve language extensions are **only** available in global scripts. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.3. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/2.3/settings/pigeonhole/#pigeonhole_setting-sieve_global_extensions>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.plugin.sieve_pipe_bin_dir` | `null or absolute path` | Points to a directory where the plugin looks for programs (shell scripts) to execute directly and pipe messages to for the *vnd.dovecot.pipe* extension. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.3. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/2.3/configuration_manual/sieve/plugins/extprograms/#configuration>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.plugin.sieve_plugins` | `null or (list of string)` | List of Sieve plugins to load. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.3. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/2.3/settings/pigeonhole/#pigeonhole_setting-sieve_plugins>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.sendmail_path` | `absolute path` | The binary to use for sending email. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#sendmail_path>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.sieve_global_extensions` | `null or (attribute set of boolean) or list of string` | Which Sieve language extensions are **only** available in global scripts. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.4. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#sieve_global_extensions>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.sieve_pipe_bin_dir` | `null or absolute path` | Points to a directory where the plugin looks for programs (shell scripts) to execute directly and pipe messages to for the *vnd.dovecot.pipe* extension. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.4. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#sieve_plugins>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.sieve_plugins` | `null or (attribute set of boolean) or list of string` | List of Sieve plugins to load. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.4. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#sieve_plugins>. |
| `services.dovecot2.settings.sieve_script_bin_path` | `null or string` | Points to the directory where the compiled binaries for this script location are stored. This directory is created automatically if possible. This option is exclusive to Dovecot 2.4. See <https://doc.dovecot.org/latest/core/summaries/settings.html#sieve_script_bin_path>. |
| `services.dovecot2.showPAMFailure` | `boolean` | Whether to enable showing the PAM failure message on authentication error (useful for OTPW). |
| `services.dovecot2.sieve.extensions` | `list of string` | Sieve extensions for use in user scripts |
| `services.dovecot2.sieve.globalExtensions` | `list of string` | Sieve extensions for use in global scripts |
| `services.dovecot2.sieve.pipeBins` | `list of absolute path` | Programs available for use by the vnd.dovecot.pipe extension |
| `services.dovecot2.sieve.scripts` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Sieve scripts to be executed. Key is a sequence, e.g. 'before2', 'after' etc. |
